Headline: Sánchez dio a Marruecos 400 coches policiales y 90 cámaras mientras escatima medios a la Guardia Civil
Article_Text:
En esa entrega, el Gobierno regaló a la Policía marroquí material valorado en 26 millones de euros.

Jucil lleva años advirtiendo de que los vehículos y el material de la Guardia Civil está viejo y obsoleto. 

La falta de medios. Desde el sindicato Jucil recordaron además que estos vehículos "no cumplen con las exigencias de seguridad".  

En este último año, hemos podido ver cómo la falta de material entre los miembros de la Guardia Civil ha estado repercutiendo de manera bastante clara sobre su esencia. Desde el pasado año, la Guardia Civil no solo ha recibido una escasa ayuda del Gobierno, sino que además sus medios han estado exhaustos. 

Por ejemplo, las cámaras del servicio son obsoletas, a la Ellabana informa que algunos miembros del servicio han dejado de atender a sus programas. 

La cuestión especialmente grave es, precisamente, el estado de los vehículos, sobre todo en la Guardia Civil, la que se encarga de luchar contra el narcotráfico en el sur de España. A raíz de la compra de todo este material a la Policía de Marruecos, el sindicato mayoritario de la Benemérita, Jucil, difundió la foto que le hizo entonces un agente al cuentakilómetros de su vehículo «al iniciar el servicio». Marcaba 430.766 kilómetros. El agente comentaba con ironía que la compra de vehículos a la policía de Marruecos es «cosa de prioridades».
Narrative: The article critiques the Spanish government's decision to provide Morocco with police vehicles while neglecting the resources necessary for the Spanish Civil Guard, highlighting a perceived misallocation of priorities.

Headline: It’s ‘time to stop using terms like long COVID,’ health agency says: ‘Unnecessary fear’
Article_Text:
They wrongly imply there is something unique and exceptional about longer-term symptoms associated with this virus,” he explained.

“This terminology can cause unnecessary fear, and in some cases, hyper-vigilance to longer symptoms that can impede recovery,” Gerrard warned.

Researchers at Queensland Health surveyed 5,112 symptom sufferers age 18 and older to reach their conclusions. 

No evidence was found that adults who tested positive in 2022 were experiencing this increased level of impairment at a higher rate than those who tested negative, or those who simply had the flu.

The study noted that rates of diagnosed “long COVID” were lower than in other countries, due to tight restrictions imposed by the Australian government during the pandemic.

The finished document is due to be presented next month at 2024’s European Congress of Clinical Microbiology and Infectious Diseases in Barcelona.

“In health systems with highly vaccinated populations, long COVID may have appeared to be a distinct and severe illness because of high volumes of COVID-19 cases during the pandemic,” Gerrard said.
Narrative: The article conveys a message questioning the use of the term "long COVID," suggesting that the fear associated with it is exaggerated and may lead to unnecessary anxiety among patients.

Headline: Trump's Old Speech After Obama and Hillary Created ISIS
Article_Text:
The article claims that former American President Donald Trump made a statement about Russia after attacking Moscow, stating that Barack Obama and Hillary Clinton established ISIS.

It also mentions that Trump revealed details about a Moscow attack, stating that a mask under the "Krokus" festival organized by U.S. intelligence agencies was directly related to the Islamic state.

An analysis from Raksrinkavanje.ba concluded that even if Trump did say this, it was a metaphor; it was later emphasized in a post on social media.

It was also determined that the claim was inaccurate and that the message directed at Putin after the massacre in Moscow did not reach his voice during the invasion of Moscow (March 22) and publication of the article.

Controversial quotes are noted in this context as bringing some coverage on the transfer of information from Moscow asking if they would justify the actions of Putin after the attack after it leads to future wars. 

There is a focus on how world consequences stem from Putin's decisions, which raises a major concern about global peace and security as costs rise due to war.
Narrative: The narrative presents a critical stance towards Trump, suggesting he misrepresents information about ISIS and his relationship with Russia, possibly undermining national security and global stability.

Headline: CORRECTION: EU does not prohibit white goods
Article_Text:
This practically means a gradual ban on the market for appliances that contain superpowerful greenhouse gases, with a cessation of servicing such devices until the full ban is in place by 2050. However, it was written in the published text that the decision needs to be approved if desired, and if approved, it will need to be formally published in the Official Journal of the EU before it comes into force. 

Mondo reported in the notable text that it will be forbidden to prohibit white goods, but it is not accurate, as even a complete ban on F-gases will not lead to a ban on white goods being destroyed, as there are different alternatives that do not destroy the ozone layer.
Narrative: The article clarifies misconceptions regarding EU regulations on appliances, emphasizing that while some gases will be banned, it won't result in a complete prohibition of white goods.

Headline: Is Iran going to attack Israel? Tehran sent a warning to Washington
Article_Text:
American officials have stated that Iran cannot attack American forces unless they collaborate with Israel in retaliation for the Iranian response to the attack on the Israeli consulate in Damascus two weeks ago.

Iran also stated that it holds the government of Washington responsible for the Israeli attack, which killed seven members of the Iranian Revolutionary Guard, including a general, mainly in the Syrian capital, regardless of American actions that try to distance themselves from that attack.

Portal Axios reported on Friday, citing American officials, that Tehran has warned Arab countries that it will target American bases in the region if Washington intervenes following any Iranian attack on Israel, as retaliation for the attack on the Iranian consulate in Damascus two weeks ago.

Israeli media report that they will include Iranian attacks on their locations in Iran, but are prepared to face every possible attack. They also stress that if Iran strikes, “we have prior warning, allowing for preparation and preemptive action.”

These statements come as the world is waiting for an Iranian response to an Israeli attack on the consulate in Damascus, amid international investigations into Iran’s possible escalation of conflicts in the region.

Headline: CORRECTION: The viral photograph of the fifteen-year-old Macron and Brigitte is a montage
Article_Text: We correct the original inaccurate version of the text that stated a viral photograph circulated on social networks shows fifteen-year-old Macron and Brigitte. In fact, the original photograph, which was circulating on social networks, features French President Emmanuel Macron beside his current wife, Brigitte Macron, when he was a teenager. The viral photograph is actually a montage derived from two frames in a documentary film "Emmanuel Macron, La stratégie du météore" (Emmanuel Macron, Meteor Strategy). Through this correction, we reaffirm the claims made in the original text.
Narrative: The article aims to clarify misinformation regarding a viral photograph of Emmanuel Macron as a teenager with Brigitte, reaffirming that it is a manipulated image meant to address fake news surrounding the couple's past.

In fact, Fernández has rebuffed the reply regarding the investiture of Jaume Collboni, as he considers that the mayor has "broken the agreement" by "having left the separatist pact", in reference to the agreements reached with Esquerra Republicana for significant budgets. "If we give them the PSC, then continue governing with ERC or Junts, it will not serve us," affirmed the candidate of the popular party.
